content.text:
# What's 42?

The number **42** is most famously known as

citations.cited_text: 42 is the answer to the “ultimate question of life, the universe, and everything,” a joke in Douglas Adams’s 1979 novel, The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the...
citations.title: 42 Meaning & Origin | Slang by Dictionary.com

content.text: the answer to the "ultimate question of life, the universe, and everything," a joke in Douglas Adams's 1979 novel, The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy.
## The Hitchhiker's Guide Reference

citations.cited_text: The number 42 is, in The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy by Douglas Adams, the "Answer to the Ultimate Question of Life, the Universe, and Everything...
citations.title: 42 (number) - Wikipedia

content.text: In The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy by Douglas Adams, 42 is the "Answer to the Ultimate Question of Life, the Universe, and Everything", calculated by an enormous supercomputer named Deep Thought over a period of 7.5 million years. Unfortunately, no one knows what the question is.

citations.cited_text: Deep Thought, however, doesn’t know the exact question it was asked to answer, rendering 42 a completely meaningless response. Deep Thought does know,...
citations.title: 42 Meaning & Origin | Slang by Dictionary.com

content.text: Deep Thought doesn't know the exact question it was asked to answer, rendering 42 a completely meaningless response. Deep Thought does know, though, that another supercomputer, known as Earth, was to be designed (by Deep Thought himself) to come up with the question in 10-million years.

### Why 42?

According to Adams himself, there's no hidden meaning.

citations.cited_text: It was a joke. It had to be a number, an ordinary, smallish number, and I chose that one. Binary representations, base 13, Tibetan monks are all compl...
citations.title: 42 Meaning & Origin | Slang by Dictionary.com

content.text: "It was a joke. It had to be a number, an ordinary, smallish number, and I chose that one. Binary representations, base 13, Tibetan monks are all complete nonsense. I sat at my desk, stared into the garden and thought '42 will do.'"

## Mathematical Properties

Despite Adams's disclaimer, 42 has several interesting mathematical properties:

-

citations.cited_text: 42 is a pronic number, an abundant number as well as a highly abundant number, a sphenic number, a practical number, and a Catalan number.
citations.title: 42 (number) - Wikipedia

content.text: 42 is a pronic number, an abundant number as well as a highly abundant number, a sphenic number, a practical number, and a Catalan number.

citations.cited_text: In the binary system, or base 2, 42 is written as 101010, which is pretty simple and, incidentally, prompted a few fans to hold parties on October 10,...
citations.title: For Math Fans: A Hitchhiker's Guide to the Number 42 | Scientific American

content.text: In the binary system, or base 2, 42 is written as 101010, which is pretty simple and, incidentally, prompted a few fans to hold parties on October 10, 2010 (10/10/10).

citations.cited_text: In one instance, the series suggests that 42 is the answer to the question “What do you get if you multiply six by nine?” That idea seems absurd becau...
citations.title: For Math Fans: A Hitchhiker's Guide to the Number 42 | Scientific American

content.text: In one instance, the series suggests that 42 is the answer to the question "What do you get if you multiply six by nine?" That idea seems absurd because 6 x 9 = 54. But in base 13, the number expressed as "42" is equal to (4 x 13) + 2 = 54.

## The Sum of Three Cubes Problem

42 was famously the last number under 100 to be solved in a notable math problem.

citations.cited_text: This sum of three cubes puzzle, first set in 1954 at the University of Cambridge and known as the Diophantine Equation x3+y3+z3=k, challenged mathemat...
citations.title: The answer to life, the universe, and everything | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology

content.text: The sum of three cubes puzzle, first set in 1954 at the University of Cambridge and known as the Diophantine Equation x³+y³+z³=k, challenged mathematicians to find solutions for numbers 1-100. All were eventually solved, or proved unsolvable, using various techniques and supercomputers, except for two numbers: 33 and 42.

citations.cited_text: Thanks to a generous offer from UK-based Charity Engine, Booker and Sutherland were able to tap into the computing power from over 400,000 volunteers’...
citations.title: The answer to life, the universe, and everything | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology

content.text: Thanks to a generous offer from UK-based Charity Engine, Booker and Sutherland were able to tap into the computing power from over 400,000 volunteers' home PCs

to solve it in 2019.

## Cultural & Other References

- **Baseball**:

citations.cited_text: The jersey number of Jackie Robinson, which is the only number retired by all Major League Baseball teams.
citations.title: 42 (number) - Wikipedia

content.text: The jersey number of Jackie Robinson, which is the only number retired by all Major League Baseball teams.
- **Google Easter Egg**:

citations.cited_text: Google also has a calculator easter egg when one searches "the answer to the ultimate question of life, the universe, and everything." Once typed (all...
citations.title: 42 (number) - Wikipedia

content.text: Google has a calculator easter egg when one searches "the answer to the ultimate question of life, the universe, and everything." Once typed (all in lowercase), the calculator answers with the number 42.
- **Japanese Culture**:

citations.cited_text: In Japanese culture, the number 42 is considered unlucky because the numerals when pronounced separately—shi ni (four two)—sound like the word "dying"...
citations.title: 42 (number) - Wikipedia

content.text: In Japanese culture, the number 42 is considered unlucky because the numerals when pronounced separately—shi ni (four two)—sound like the word "dying"
- **Kabbalah**:

citations.cited_text: ... 42 is the number with which God creates the Universe in Kabbalistic tradition.
citations.title: 42 (number) - Wikipedia

content.text: 42 is the number with which God creates the Universe in Kabbalistic tradition.
- **Chemistry**:

citations.cited_text: The element ‘Molybdenum’ has an atomic number of 42.
citations.title: 42 Facts about 42! – Math Tools

content.text: The element 'Molybdenum' has an atomic number of 42.
- **Marathon**:

citations.cited_text: A marathon is 42,195 meters long. However, it was 42 kilometers originally.
citations.title: 42 Facts about 42! – Math Tools

content.text: A marathon is 42,195 meters long. However, it was 42 kilometers originally.
In short, 42 has become

citations.cited_text: The author’s choice of the number 42 has become a fixture of geek culture. It’s at the origin of a multitude of jokes and winks exchanged between init...
citations.title: For Math Fans: A Hitchhiker's Guide to the Number 42 | Scientific American

content.text: a fixture of geek culture, at the origin of a multitude of jokes and winks exchanged between initiates.
